goal: Document that BP-C2 is green after BP-091, BP-092, and BP-093, and define the safe BP-C3 entry gate without starting BP-C3 implementation.
claims: BP-C2 has three green modules: Multi-model Pool, Context Broker, and WLP Council Session Guard.
BP-C3 may be prepared only after this checkpoint and with new WLP contracts.
BP-C3 modules remain blocked from direct implementation in BP-094.
Known BP-C2 limits are documented: no provider API calls, no architecture digest, no excluded_context repulsion beyond broker path blocks.
assumptions: BP-091, BP-092, and BP-093 are already pushed to main.
Green means local deterministic checks pass, not production/live provider readiness.
BP-C3 should start with cautious contracts because worktrees, memory, and AICOS references carry higher risk.
dod: The checkpoint lists BP-C2 green evidence and known limits.
The checkpoint blocks BP-C3 implementation without new WLP contracts.
prior_task_findings: BP-091 added route-only Model Pool, BP-092 added read-only Context Broker, and BP-093 added optional Council Session Guard.
